description: phiC31_attP — 100 bp phage attachment site (attP) of Streptomyces phage phiC31, recognized by the phiC31 large serine integrase; contains the minimal 39 bp core with two 9 bp inverted-repeat half-sites and the central TT crossover.

references.title: Structural analysis of the actinophage phi C31 attachment site
In vitro site-specific integration of bacteriophage DNA catalyzed by a recombinase of the resolvase/invertase family

references.authors: Smith MC, Burns RN, Wilson SE, Gregory MA
references.title: The complete genome sequence of the Streptomyces temperate phage phiC31: evolutionary relationships to other viruses

references.authors: Groth AC, Olivares EC, Thyagarajan B, Calos MP
references.title: A phage integrase directs efficient site-specific integration in human cells

references.authors: St-Pierre F, Cui L, Priest DG, Endy D, Dodd IB, Shearwin KE
references.title: One-step cloning and chromosomal integration of DNA

label: phiC31_attP
note: Phage attachment site (attP) of Streptomyces temperate phage phiC31, the DNA target recognized by the phiC31 large serine integrase. 100 bp spanning the attP crossover region; exact reverse complement of X57036.1 positions 1590-1689. The phiC31 integrase recombines this attP with a chromosomal attB to integrate DNA irreversibly and unidirectionally, with no required host accessory factors. Widely used as a chromosomal landing pad in heterologous hosts including mammalian cells. The functional center comprises two 9 bp imperfect inverted-repeat integrase half-sites flanking a spacer that carries the central TT crossover dinucleotide.

label: phiC31 attP minimal core (39 bp)
note: Minimal functional phiC31 attP defined as 39 bp by systematic truncation assay (Groth et al. 2000, PMID 10801973). Spans positions 31-70 of the 100 bp part and contains both 9 bp inverted-repeat integrase half-sites plus the central TT crossover; the ~31 bp flanking the core on each side lie outside the minimal functional site.

label: phiC31 Int binding -- left half-site
note: 9 bp imperfect inverted-repeat left half-site (CCCCAACTG), one of the two integrase binding determinants of phiC31 attP (Groth et al. 2000, PMID 10801973). The left and right half-sites are in inverted orientation flanking the spacer that carries the TT crossover. Half-site boundary is a figure-level detail in the source; sequence and coordinates verified against the stored part.

label: phiC31 Int binding -- right half-site
note: 9 bp imperfect inverted-repeat right half-site (Groth et al. 2000, PMID 10801973). On the plus strand reads CAGTTGGGG; its reverse complement is CCCCAACTG, the mirror of the left half-site, confirming the inverted-repeat architecture. Annotated on the minus strand to reflect its inverted orientation relative to the left half-site. Half-site boundary is a figure-level detail in the source; sequence and coordinates verified against the stored part.

label: TT crossover dinucleotide
note: Central TT overlap dinucleotide at the recombination crossover point of phiC31 attP, shared with attB; lies near the center of the minimal 39 bp core. phiC31 integrase is a large serine recombinase of the resolvase/invertase family (Thorpe & Smith 1998, PMID 9576912), a family that makes staggered double-strand cuts leaving a short central overlap, so the 2 bp stagger is mechanistically expected. The specific TT identity at this position is consistent with the X57036.1 crossover annotation (attP crossover at X57036 positions 1639-1641) but is not explicitly attributed to a single primary reference here; treat the exact 2 bp identity as consensus/inferred pending a junction-sequencing primary source.

provenance.sequence_source: X57036.1 (Bacteriophage Phi-C31 attP phage attachment site crossover region and 3 ORF's), reverse complement of positions 1590-1689, 100% identity over 100 bp

functional_claims.label: phiC31 attP is the integrase target recognized by the phiC31 large serine integrase, which recombines attP x attB to integrate DNA unidirectionally with no required host accessory factors.

value.directionality: unidirectional (excision requires a recombination directionality factor)

source.quote: In vitro site-specific integration of bacteriophage DNA catalyzed by a recombinase of the resolvase/invertase family
source.section: in vitro integration with purified integrase

functional_claims.label: Minimal functional phiC31 attP is 39 bp, defined by systematic truncation assay.

value.architecture: two 9 bp inverted-repeat integrase half-sites flanking a spacer containing the central crossover

source.quote: defines minimal phiC31 attB and attP by truncation; a phage integrase directs efficient site-specific integration in human cells

functional_claims.label: phiC31 integrase plus this attP function across a broad host range, including efficient site-specific integration in mammalian (human) cells.

value.native_host: Streptomyces (phiC31 is a Streptomyces temperate phage)

source.quote: A phage integrase directs efficient site-specific integration in human cells
